***************
5-Bullet Friday
***************

 

The latest from author and investor Tim Ferriss (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/dpheh0heq7mz6ram/aHR0cHM6Ly90aW0uYmxvZy8=
)

 

 

Hi All!

 
Here is your weekly dose of 5-Bullet Friday, a list of what I’m
pondering and exploring. I threw in an extra bullet for some
spice.

 
This edition is brought to you by COYOTE (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/e0hph7h7kn29vmt8/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9FeHBsb2RpbmctS2l0dGVucy1GZXJyaXNzLVByZXNlbnQtTWludXRlcy9kcC9CMEZCVDg4VlJKLw==
), the hit card game I created with Exploding Kittens. 10 minutes
to play, roughly $10 to get, and 10,000 ways it can make you
laugh. The game is currently #1 in its category on Amazon. Check
it out here (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/e0hph7h7kn29vmt8/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9FeHBsb2RpbmctS2l0dGVucy1GZXJyaXNzLVByZXNlbnQtTWludXRlcy9kcC9CMEZCVDg4VlJKLw==
). Non-U.S. friends, please see the P.S. at the bottom of this
email.

 
And now, here are a few goodies from this past week…

 

------------------------
Interview I’m revisiting
------------------------

​My Next Guest with David Letterman and Shah Rukh Khan (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/7qh7h8h904mvlqiz/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmV0ZmxpeC5jb20vdGl0bGUvODExMjEwMjM=
). If you know who Shah Rukh Khan (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/owhkhqhw487399bv/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaW5zdGFncmFtLmNvbS9pYW1zcms=
) is, you’ll want to see this. If you don’t know who Shah Rukh
Khan is, you will especially want to see this. The scale and
charm of everything about SRK is truly mind-boggling. He’s a
consummate pro.

 

---------------------
Blog post I’m reading
---------------------

“Everything I Know about Self-Publishing (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/z2hghnheod896xip/aHR0cHM6Ly9ray5vcmcvdGhldGVjaG5pdW0vZXZlcnl0aGluZy1pLWtub3ctYWJvdXQtc2VsZi1wdWJsaXNoaW5nLw==
)” by Kevin Kelly (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/p8heh9h497lk60sq/aHR0cHM6Ly90aW0uYmxvZy8yMDIzLzA0LzI2L2tldmluLWtlbGx5LWV4Y2VsbGVudC1hZHZpY2UtZm9yLWxpdmluZy8_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTViZiZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWNvbnZlcnRraXQmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1jb252ZXJ0a2l0
). Kevin is the founding editor of WIRED magazine and a listener
favorite on the podcast. His latest blog post on self-publishing
covers practically everything: going the traditional route,
on-demand, digital, distribution, crowd-funding, promotion,
subscriptions, and more. Here’s the introduction:
​
​In my professional life, I’ve had several bestselling books
published by New York publishers, as well as many other titles
that sold modestly. I have also self-published a bunch of books,
including one bestseller on Amazon and two massive hit
Kickstarter-funded books. I have had lots of foreign edition
books released by other publishers around the world, including
bestsellers in those countries. Every year I also publish a few
private books to give away. I’ve contracted books to be printed
in the US and overseas. I’ve sold big coffee-table masterpieces
and tiny text booklets. Together with partners, I run some
notable newsletters, a very popular website, and a podcast with
420 episodes. I accumulated followers on various platforms. I’m
often asked for advice about how to go about publishing today,
with all its options, so here is everything I have learned about
publishing and self-publishing so far.

 

--------------------------
Short audiobook I’m loving
--------------------------

​Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/dpheh0heq7mz6lcm/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXVkaWJsZS5jb20vcGQvQ2hhcmxpZS1hbmQtdGhlLUNob2NvbGF0ZS1GYWN0b3J5LUF1ZGlvYm9vay9CMDBEOEo1SU1V
) by Roald Dahl (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/e0hph7h7kn29vri8/aHR0cHM6Ly9lbi53aWtpcGVkaWEub3JnL3dpa2kvUm9hbGRfRGFobA==
), read by Douglas Hodge (
https://click.fourhourmail.
). After finishing When Breath Becomes Air (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/owhkhqhw48739esv/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9XaGVuLUJyZWF0aC1CZWNvbWVzLVBhdWwtS2FsYW5pdGhpLWVib29rL2RwL0IwMFhTU1lSNTA_cmVmXz1hc3RfYXV0aG9yX21wYg==
) by Paul Kalanithi, I needed a light palate cleanser. This is
what I chose.
​
Did you know that, in addition to selling something like 300
million copies of his children’s books worldwide, Roald Dahl was
an intelligence officer and ace fighter pilot? But back to the
book, it only struck me in the mountains of Montana two weeks
ago, as friends and I were trying to remember the brat girl’s
name from Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (Veruca Salt), that I
was flying blind. I have watched a lot of films based on Roald’s
books (Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/z2hghnheod896ghp/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9XaWxseS1Xb25rYS1DaG9jb2xhdGUtRmFjdG9yeS1XaWxkZXIvZHAvQjAwMllOR05HNi8=
), The BFG (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/p8heh9h497lkoktq/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9CRkctVGhlYXRyaWNhbC1WZXJzaW9uLU1hcmstUnlsYW5jZS9kcC9CMDFIUVVWQlVFLw==
), James and the Giant Peach (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/x0hph6hen7l80ri5/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucHJpbWV2aWRlby5jb20vZHAvYW16bjEuZHYuZ3RpLjJlYjdkYjUxLTE5M2MtNmE2OC02MDM1LWI2MTJkMDY1MDQxOT9hdXRvcGxheT0wJnJlZl89YXR2X2NmX3N0cmdfd2I=
), Fantastic Mr. Fox (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/6qheh8hlp2dve5co/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9ncC92aWRlby9kZXRhaWwvQjAwQlVVTDRTRy9yZWY9YXR2X3NyX2ZsZV9jX3NyNGU4ZmZiXzFfMV8xP3BhZ2VUeXBlSWQ9QjAwMzc4VkdHTyZwYWdlVHlwZUlkU291cmNlPUFTSU4mcWlkPTE3NTU5MDYzMTg1NDUmc3I9MS0x
), etc.), but I haven’t actually read the books that started it
all! So I started with Charlie, and the voice performance by
Douglas Hodge made it a joy to listen to.

 

----------------------------
Science I’m tracking closely
----------------------------

“Why Mitochondria Are More Like a Motherboard Than the Powerhouse
of the Cell (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/kkhmh6hn893gvetl/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2NpZW50aWZpY2FtZXJpY2FuLmNvbS9hcnRpY2xlL3doeS1taXRvY2hvbmRyaWEtYXJlLW1vcmUtbGlrZS1hLW1vdGhlcmJvYXJkLXRoYW4tdGhlLXBvd2VyaG91c2Utb2YtdGhlLWNlbGwv
)” (Scientific American). Alternate link:
https://archive.is/OiSlu (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/58hvh7hg59zp2ot6/aHR0cHM6Ly9hcmNoaXZlLmlzL09pU2x1
). From the article:
​
​All well and good, you may think. What does all this mean for my
health or how long I’m going to live?
​
The short answer is that it may have everything to do with human
health. Diabetes, neurodegenerative conditions, cancer and even
mental health illnesses are all emerging as metabolic disorders
involving malfunctioning mitochondria. And these findings are
indicating new routes for intervention.
​
​...
​
​When cells are injured or stressed, they may leak mtDNA into the
cell’s interior, the cytoplasm, or even into the blood. Along
with Caroline Trumpff of Columbia, Anna Marsland and Brett
Kaufman of the University of Pittsburgh, and other co-workers, I
found that mental stress induced by having to speak in public for
five minutes increased the amount of free-floating mtDNA in the
blood. People in intensive care units who are grievously ill tend
to have very high levels of mtDNA in their blood. Because mtDNA
rings resemble bacterial DNA, immune cells see them as pathogens
and mount an attack that can develop into inflammation. And
inflammation, clinicians know well, is linked to the onset and
progression of a host of chronic health conditions [including
depression].

 

--------------------------------------------------------------
Device I’m using for anti-inflammation and tissue decongestion
--------------------------------------------------------------

​​Marc Pro | Recovery Tool for Sore Muscles & Pain Relief (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/25h2hoh37oerwei3/aHR0cHM6Ly9tYXJjcHJvLmNvbS9wcm9kdWN0L21hcmMtcHJvLw==
). Kelly Starrett (@thereadystate (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/qvh8h7hd860gp6il/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaW5zdGFncmFtLmNvbS90aGVyZWFkeXN0YXRlLz9obD1lbg==
)) of The Ready State (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/g3hnh5hmezk7weur/aHR0cHM6Ly90aGVyZWFkeXN0YXRlLmNvbS8=
) originally recommended the Marc Pro to me for decreasing
swelling after a snowboarding accident years ago. Or maybe it was
when he tricked me into a 280-mile mountain bike ride that he
promised would be “like a beach cruiser in Santa Monica”? Either
way, it works, and both he and a top MLB physio and performance
coach recommended I use it for hours a day after my elbow surgery
this week.

Book highlight I’m pondering
----------------------------

“Psychedelic experiences are usually not about visiting some
transcendent realm of angels and demons (though that does
happen); more often than not, they are about experiencing the
here and now in a very intense way.”
​— Dennis McKenna (
https://click.fourhourmail.com/75u0qzo2qkh8h6ow0vxsniwlrvr66tnhz7r/n2hohvhvnokw06b6/aHR0cHM6Ly90aW0uYmxvZy8yMDIxLzA3LzIxL2Rlbm5pcy1tY2tlbm5hLz91dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249NWJmJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09Y29udmVydGtpdCZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWNvbnZlcnRraXQ=
